attacking_style: Aggressive descending tactics deployed in mid-race (around 85km), using technical skills and challenging conditions to force splits rather than traditional climbing attacks.
team_tactics: Insufficient data to establish clear team tactical patterns from single race observation.
terrain_preference: Technical descents, especially in damp/challenging conditions where bike handling skills can fracture the peloton. Exposed on final climbs where splits get reabsorbed.
vulnerability: Attacks absorbed on subsequent climbing stages; lacks ability to sustain splits created on descents when faced with sustained uphill finishes.
key_patterns: Exploits technical descents to initiate race-splitting moves in mid-race phases.
Utilizes adverse weather conditions (damp roads) to maximize advantage on descents.
Creates separation through aggressive cornering rather than pure power output.
form_trajectory: unknown: single race observation insufficient to determine trajectory or consistency of form
